AGBPage.title: User Agreement - General Terms and Conditions (GTC) of the mysmart.health Platform

section1.title: § 1 Subject of the Agreement
section1.text1: The provider "Future Health GmbH, Wildenbruchstr. 13, 40545 Düsseldorf - District Court Düsseldorf, HRB / 72666, represented by Managing Director Dr. Ferdinand Jeute, Email jeute@future-health.de" operates the digital platform mysmart.health, which provides companies and organizations with a presentation and digital health services.
section1.text2: The subject of this agreement is the time-limited integration and use of the platform by the partner for the following purposes:
list: Digital presentation and marketing presence of the partner company on the platform,
Access to digital content and health services from mysmart.health,
Participation in mysmart.health initiatives and digital events.

section2.title: § 2 Contract Start and Duration
text: The contract comes into force upon registration of the participant by both parties.
It is concluded for an indefinite period and can be terminated monthly.
Termination must be made in writing or in text form (email is sufficient) at least 14 days before the end of the month.
The following information is required to set up an administrator account:
text2: Upon registration, the participant's administrator gains access to the administration area of Future Health or their own Future Health pages. The administrator account data can be changed at any time in the account settings.
For subsequent logins, the stored email address and password must be entered. Changing the password is only possible in the account settings.
The owner or administrator is responsible for securely storing the account credentials. The licensor is not liable for any damages resulting from loss.

section3.title: § 3 Remuneration
text: The use of the platform is free of charge from the start of the contract.
Additional services are charged according to the current price list or the individually agreed monthly fee.
Payments are due annually in advance without deduction.
The provider is entitled to adjust prices with a notice period of 30 days to the end of the month.

section4.title: § 4 Services of the Provider
section4.text: The provider grants the partner access to the mysmart.health platform and enables the use of its functions. The provider ensures technical availability of at least 95% monthly. Content and technical changes for further development are reserved.

section5.title: § 5 Obligations of the Partner
section5.text: The partner undertakes to use the platform exclusively within the scope of the contractual purpose. The partner is responsible for the content they publish. The partner may not publish unlawful or discriminatory content. Brands, logos, and materials of mysmart.health may only be used within this cooperation.

section6.title: § 6 Usage Rights
section6.text: The provider grants the partner a simple, non-transferable right to use the platform for the duration of the contract. Sharing access or content with third parties is prohibited. All rights to the platform, content, and designs remain with the provider.

section7.title: § 7 Liability
section7.text: The provider is only liable in cases of intent and gross negligence. No liability is assumed for data loss or indirect damages. Liability under the Product Liability Act remains unaffected.

section8.title: § 8 Data Protection
section8.text: Both parties undertake to comply with data protection regulations in accordance with the GDPR. Personal data is processed exclusively for the purpose of fulfilling the contract. Further details are governed by a separate data processing agreement (DPA), if required.

section9.title: § 9 Confidentiality
section9.text: Both parties undertake to treat all confidential information obtained under this agreement as strictly confidential and not to disclose it to third parties.

section10.title: § 10 Final Provisions
text: Amendments and additions to this contract must be made in writing.
If individual provisions are invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions remains unaffected.
German law applies, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods.

eu.text2: Future Health is neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

PrivacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
PrivacyPolicy.intro: We appreciate your interest in our company and our website. The protection of your personal data is very important to us. Below we inform you about the processing of personal data when using our website.

section3.title: 3. Legal Basis
section3.text: We process personal data exclusively in accordance with the provisions of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the Federal Data Protection Act (BDSG), and the Telecommunications-Telemedia Data Protection Act (TTDSG).

section4.title: 4. Processing of Personal Data When Visiting the Website
section4.intro: When you access our website, the following data is automatically transmitted by your browser to our server and stored in log files:
section4.text: Purpose: Ensuring functionality, system security, error analysis. Legal basis: Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest). Data will be deleted after a maximum of 14 days.

section5.title: 5. Cookies and Consent Management
section5.text1: Our website uses cookies and similar technologies.
section5.text2: Essential cookies: necessary for the operation of the website (e.g. shopping cart, login).
section5.text3: Statistics, marketing or convenience cookies are only set with your explicit consent (§ 25 para. 1 TTDSG in conjunction with Art. 6 para. 1 lit. a GDPR).
section5.text4: When you first visit our website, we show you a consent banner where you can choose which cookies you want to allow. You can change this selection at any time in the cookie settings.

section6.title: 6. Services and Tools Used
list: d) Social media plugins: [Facebook, LinkedIn, Instagram]

section7.title: 7. Contact
section7.text1: If you contact us via contact form or email, your data (e.g. name, email address, message content) will be stored for processing.
section7.text2: The legal basis for data processing is Art. 6 para. 1 lit. a GDPR (consent) for voluntarily activated cookies and Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest) for essential cookies.
section7.text3: You can revoke your consent at any time in the cookie settings.

section8.title: 8. Storage Duration
section8.text: We store personal data only as long as necessary to fulfill the respective purpose or as required by statutory retention periods.

section9.title: 9. Rights of Data Subjects
section9.text: You have the following rights at any time: access, rectification, erasure, restriction, data portability, objection, withdrawal, and the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.

section10.title: 10. Automated Decision-Making
section10.text: Automated decision-making or profiling does not take place.
